user_message: Can you work with me on executive brief for Alpine Ski House?

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-02-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on executive brief from our last session.

[00:07:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:13:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:20:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:27:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-G trap (Skips action-item due dates when discussed); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-G failure mode (Skips action-item due dates when discussed)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-G -- Skips action-item due dates when discussed
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
